sections.label: Section 1
sections.original: Εἴθ ’ ὤφελ ’ Ἀργοῦς μὴ διαπτάσθαι σκάφος
sections.translation: Ah ! would to Heaven the good ship Argo ne ’ er had sped its course to the Colchian land through the misty blue Symplegades , nor ever in the glens of Pelion the pine been felled to furnish with oars the chieftain ’ s hands ,

sections.label: Section 5
sections.original: ἀνδρῶν ἀρίστων , οἳ τὸ πάγχρυσον δέρας
sections.translation: who went to fetch the golden fleece for Pelias ; for then would my own mistress Medea never have sailed to the turrets of Iolcos , her soul with love for Jason smitten , nor would she have beguiled the daughters of Pelias

sections.label: Section 10
sections.original: πατέρα κατῴκει τήνδε γῆν Κορινθίαν
sections.translation: to slay their father and come to live here in the land of Corinth with her husband and children , where her exile found favour with the citizens to whose land she had come , and in all things of her own accord was she at one with Jason , the greatest safeguard this

sections.label: Section 15
sections.original: ὅταν γυνὴ πρὸς ἄνδρα μὴ διχοστατῇ .
sections.translation: when wife and husband do agree ; but now their love is all turned to hate , and tenderest ties are weak . For Jason hath betrayed his own children and my mistress dear for the love of a royal bride , for he hath wedded the daughter of Creon , lord of this land .

sections.label: Section 20
sections.original: Μήδεια δ ’ ἡ δύστηνος ἠτιμασμένη
sections.translation: While Medea his hapless wife , thus scorned , appeals to the oaths he swore , recalls the strong pledge his right hand gave , and bids heaven be witness what requital she is finding from Jason . And here she lies fasting , yielding her body to her grief ,

sections.label: Section 25
sections.original: τὸν πάντα συντήκουσα δακρύοις χρόνον ,
sections.translation: wasting away in tears ever since she learnt that she was wronged by her husband , never lifting her eye nor raising her face from off the ground ; and she lends as deaf an ear to her friend ’ s warning as if she were a rock or ocean billow , save when she turns her snow-white neck aside

sections.label: Section 30
sections.original: ἢν μή ποτε στρέψασα πάλλευκον δέρην
sections.translation: and softly to herself bemoans her father dear , her country and her home , which she gave up to come hither with the man who now holds her in dishonour . She , poor lady , hath by sad experience learnt how good a thing it is

sections.label: Section 35
sections.original: οἷον πατρῴας μὴ ἀπολείπεσθαι χθονός .
sections.translation: never to quit one ’ s native land . And she hates her children now and feels no joy at seeing them ; I am afeard she may contrive some untoward scheme ; for her mood is dangerous nor will she brook her cruel treatment ; full well I know her , and I much do dread

sections.label: Section 40
sections.original: μὴ θηκτὸν ὤσῃ φάσγανον δι ’ ἥπατος ,
sections.translation: that she will plunge the keen sword through their heart , stealing without a word into the chamber where their marriage couch is spread , or else that she will slay the prince and bridegroom too , and so find some calamity still more grievous than the present ; for dreadful is her wrath ;

sections.label: Section 45
sections.original: ἔχθραν τις αὐτῇ καλλίνικον οἴσεται .
sections.translation: verily the man that doth incur her hate will have no easy task to raise o ’ er her a song of triumph . Lo ! where her sons come hither from their childish sports ; little they reck of their mother ’ s woes , for the soul of the young is no inend to sorrow .

sections.label: Section 49
sections.original: παλαιὸν οἴκων κτῆμα δεσποίνης ἐμῆς ,
sections.translation: Why dost thou , so long my lady ’ s own handmaid ,

sections.label: Section 50
sections.original: τί πρὸς πύλαισι τήνδ ’ ἄγουσ ’ ἐρημίαν
sections.translation: stand here at the gate alone , loudly lamenting to thyself the piteous tale ? how comes it that Medea will have thee leave her to herself ?

sections.label: Section 53
sections.original: τέκνων ὀπαδὲ πρέσβυ τῶν Ἰάσονος ,
sections.translation: Old man , attendant on the sons of Jason , our masters ’ fortunes when they go awry make good slaves grieve

sections.label: Section 55
sections.original: κακῶς πίτνοντα , καὶ φρενῶν ἀνθάπτεται .
sections.translation: and touch their hearts . Oh ! I have come to such a pitch of grief that there stole a yearning wish upon me to come forth hither and proclaim to heaven and earth my mistress ’ s hard fate .

sections.label: Section 59
sections.original: οὔπω γὰρ ἡ τάλαινα παύεται γόων ;
sections.translation: What ! has not the poor lady ceased yet from her lamentation ?

sections.label: Section 60
sections.original: ζηλῶ σ ’· ἐν ἀρχῇ πῆμα κοὐδέπω μεσοῖ .
sections.translation: Would I were as thou art ! the mischief is but now beginning ; it has not reached its climax yet .

sections.label: Section 61
sections.original: ὦ μῶρος — εἰ χρὴ δεσπότας εἰπεῖν τόδε ·
sections.translation: O foolish one , if I may call my mistress such a name ; how little she recks of evils yet more recent !

sections.label: Section 63
sections.original: τί δ ’ ἔστιν , ὦ γεραιέ ; μὴ φθόνει φράσαι .
sections.translation: What mean ’ st , old man ? grudge not to tell me .

sections.label: Section 64
sections.original: οὐδέν · μετέγνων καὶ τὰ πρόσθ ’ εἰρημένα .
sections.translation: ’ Tis naught ; I do repent me even of the words I have spoken .

sections.label: Section 65
sections.original: μή , πρὸς γενείου , κρύπτε σύνδουλον σέθεν ·
sections.translation: Nay , by thy beard I conjure thee , hide it not from thy fellow-slave ; I will be silent , if need be , on that text .

sections.label: Section 67
sections.original: ἤκουσά του λέγοντος , οὐ δοκῶν κλύειν ,
sections.translation: I heard one say , pretending not to listen as I approached the place where our greybeards sit playing draughts [*] near Pirene ’ s sacred spring ,

sections.label: Section 70
sections.original: ὡς τούσδε παῖδας γῆς ἐλᾶν Κορινθίας
sections.translation: that Creon , the ruler of this land , is bent on driving these children and their mother from the boundaries of Corinth ; but I know not whether the news is to be relied upon , and would fain it were not .

sections.label: Section 74
sections.original: καὶ ταῦτ ’ Ἰάσων παῖδας ἐξανέξεται
sections.translation: What ! will Jason brook such treatment of his sons ,

sections.label: Section 75
sections.original: πάσχοντας , εἰ καὶ μητρὶ διαφορὰν ἔχει ;
sections.translation: even though he be at variance with their mother ?

sections.label: Section 76
sections.original: παλαιὰ καινῶν λείπεται κηδευμάτων ,
sections.translation: Old ties give way to new ; he bears no longer any love to this family .

sections.label: Section 78
sections.original: ἀπωλόμεσθ ’ ἄρ ’, εἰ κακὸν προσοίσομεν
sections.translation: Undone , it seems , are we , if to old woes fresh ones we add , ere we have drained the former to the dregs .

sections.label: Section 80
sections.original: ἀτὰρ σύ γ ’— οὐ γὰρ καιρὸς εἰδέναι τόδε
sections.translation: Hold thou thy peace , say not a word of this ; ’ tis no time for our mistress to learn hereof .

sections.label: Section 82
sections.original: ὦ τέκν ’, ἀκούεθ ’ οἷος εἰς ὑμᾶς πατήρ ;
sections.translation: O children , do ye hear how your father feels towards you ? Perdition catch him , but no ! he is my master still ; yet is he proved a veiy traitor to his nearest and dearest .

sections.label: Section 85
sections.original: τίς δ ’ οὐχὶ θνητῶν ; ἄρτι γιγνώσκεις τόδε ,
sections.translation: And who ’ mongst men is not ? Art learning only now , that every single man cares for himself more than for his neighbour , some from honest , motives , others for mere gain ’ s sake ? seeing that to indulge his passion their father has ceased to love these children .

sections.label: Section 89
sections.original: ἴτ ’— εὖ γὰρ ἔσται — δωμάτων ἔσω , τέκνα .
sections.translation: Go , children , within the house ; all will be well .

sections.label: Section 90
sections.original: σὺ δ ’ ὡς μάλιστα τούσδ ’ ἐρημώσας ἔχε
sections.translation: Do thou keep them as far away as may be , and bring them not near their mother in her evil hour . For ere this have I seen her eyeing them savagely , as though she were minded to do them some hurt , and well I know she will not cease from her fury till she have pounced on some victim .

sections.label: Section 95
sections.original: ἐχθρούς γε μέντοι , μὴ φίλους , δράσειέ τι .
sections.translation: At least may she turn her hand against her foes , and not against her friends .

sections.label: Section 96
sections.original: ἰώ ,
sections.translation: ( within ). Ah , me ! a wretched suffering woman I ! O would that I could die !

sections.label: Section 98
sections.original: τόδ ’ ἐκεῖνο , φίλοι παῖδες · μήτηρ
sections.translation: ’ Tis as I said , my dear children ; wild fancies stir your mother ’ s heart , wild fury goads her on .

sections.label: Section 100
sections.original: σπεύσατε θᾶσσον δώματος εἴσω
sections.translation: Into the house without delay , come not near her eye , approach her not , beware her savage mood , the fell tempest of her reckless heart .

sections.label: Section 105
sections.original: ἴτε νῦν , χωρεῖθ ’ ὡς τάχος εἴσω .—
sections.translation: In , in with what speed ye may . For ’ tis plain she will soon redouble her fury ; that cry is but the herald of the gathering storm-cloud whose lightning soon will flash ; what will her proud restless

sections.label: Section 110
sections.original: ψυχὴ δηχθεῖσα κακοῖσιν ;
sections.translation: soul , in the anguish of despair , be guilty of ? [ Exit Attendant with the children .

sections.label: Section 111
sections.original: αἰαῖ ,
sections.translation: ( within ). Ah , me ! the agony I have suffered , deep enough to call for these laments ! Curse you and your father too , ye children damned , sons of a doomed mother ! Ruin seize the whole family !

sections.label: Section 115
sections.original: ἰώ μοί μοι , ἰὼ τλήμων .
sections.translation: Ah me ! ah me ! the pity of it ! Why , pray , do thy children share their father ’ s crime ? Why hatest thou them ? Woe is you , poor children , how do I grieve for you lest ye suffer some outrage ! Strange are the tempers of princes ,

sections.label: Section 120
sections.original: ὀλίγ ’ ἀρχόμενοι , πολλὰ κρατοῦντες
sections.translation: and maybe because they seldom have to obey , and mostly lord it over others , change they their moods with difficulty . ’ Tis better then to have been trained to live on equal terms . Be it mine to reach old age , not in proud pomp , but in security !

sections.label: Section 125
sections.original: τῶν γὰρ μετρίων πρῶτα μὲν εἰπεῖν
sections.translation: Moderation wins the day first as a better word for men to use , and likewise it is far the best course for them to pursue ; but greatness that doth o ’ erreach itself , brings no blessing to mortal men ; but pays a penalty of greater ruin whenever

sections.label: Section 130
sections.original: δαίμων οἴκοις , ἀπέδωκεν .
sections.translation: fortune is wroth with a family .

sections.label: Section 131
sections.original: ἔκλυον φωνάν , ἔκλυον δὲ βοὰν
sections.translation: I heard the voice , uplifted loud , of our poor Colchian lady , nor yet is she quiet ; speak , aged dame ,

sections.label: Section 135
sections.original: ἔκλυον · οὐδὲ συνήδομαι , ὦ γύναι , ἄλγεσιν
sections.translation: for as I stood by the house with double gates I heard a voice of weeping from within , and I do grieve , lady , for the sorrows of this house , for it hath won my love .

sections.label: Section 139
sections.original: οὐκ εἰσὶ δόμοι · φροῦδα τάδ ’ ἤδη .
sections.translation: ’ Tis a house no more ; all that is passed away long since ;

sections.label: Section 140
sections.original: τὸν μὲν γὰρ ἔχει λέκτρα τυράννων ,
sections.translation: a royal bride keeps Jason at her side , while our mistress pines away in her bower , finding no comfort for her soul in aught her friends can say .

sections.label: Section 144
sections.original: αἰαῖ · ὦ Ζεῦ καὶ Γᾶ καὶ Φῶς ·
sections.translation: ( within ). Oh , oh ! Would that Heaven ’ s levin bolt would cleave this head in twain !

sections.label: Section 145
sections.original: βαίη · τί δέ μοι ζῆν ἔτι κέρδος ;
sections.translation: What gain is life to me ? Woe , woe is me ! O , to die and win release , quitting this loathed existence !

sections.label: Section 1.148
sections.original: — ἄιες · ὦ Ζεῦ καὶ γᾶ καὶ φῶς ·
sections.translation: Didst hear , O Zeus , thou earth , and thou , O light , the piteous note of woe

sections.label: Section 1.150
sections.original: μέλπει νύμφα ;
sections.translation: the hapless wife is uttering ? How shall a yearning for that insatiate [*] resting-place ever hasten for thee , poor reckless one , the end that death alone can bring ? Never pray for that .

sections.label: Section 1.155
sections.original: — εἰ δὲ σὸς πόσις
sections.translation: And if thy lord prefers a fresh love , be not angered with him for that ; Zeus will judge ’ twixt thee and him herein . Then mourn not for thy husband ’ s loss too much , nor waste thyself away .

sections.label: Section 160
sections.original: ὦ μεγάλα Θέμι καὶ πότνι ’ Ἄρτεμι
sections.translation: ( within ). Great Themis , and husband [*] of Themis , behold what I am suffering now , though I did bind that accursed one , my husband , by strong oaths to me ? O , to see him and his bride some day brought to utter destruction , they and their house with them ,

sections.label: Section 165
sections.original: οἷ ’ ἐμὲ πρόσθεν τολμῶσ ’ ἀδικεῖν .
sections.translation: for that they presume to wrong me thus unprovoked . O my father , my country , that I have left to my shame , after slaying my own brother .

sections.label: Section 168
sections.original: κλύεθ ’ οἷα λέγει κἀπιβοᾶται
sections.translation: Do ye hear her words , how loudly she adjures Themis , oft invoked , and Zeus , whom

sections.label: Section 170
sections.original: θνητοῖς ταμίας νενόμισται ;
sections.translation: men regard as keeper of their oaths ? On no mere trifle surely will our mistress spend her rage .

sections.label: Section 1.173
sections.original: — πῶς ἂν ἐς ὄψιν τὰν ἁμετέραν
sections.translation: Would that she would come forth for us to see , and

sections.label: Section 1.175
sections.original: δέξαιτ ’ ὀμφάν ;
sections.translation: listen to the words of counsel we might give , if haply she might lay aside the fierce fury of her wrath , and her temper stern . Never be my zeal at any rate denied my friends !

sections.label: Section 1.180
sections.original: — ἀλλὰ βᾶσά νιν
sections.translation: But go thou and bring her hither outside the house , and tell her this our friendly thought ; haste thee ere she do some mischief to those inside the house , for this sorrow of hers is mounting high .

sections.label: Section 184
sections.original: δράσω τάδ ’· ἀτὰρ φόβος εἰ πείσω
sections.translation: This will I do ; but I have my doubts whether I shall persuade

sections.label: Section 185
sections.original: δέσποιναν ἐμήν ·
sections.translation: my mistress ; still willingly will I undertake this trouble for you ; albeit , she glares upon her servants with the look of a lioness with cubs , whenso anyone draws nigh to speak to her .
